## Meta Campaign Blueprint: SalesIntel — VP Sales Lead Gen

### Campaign Overview
**Campaign Name:** SalesIntel | Lead Gen | VP Sales | B2B SaaS | Oct 2025
**Objective:** Lead Generation (Instant Forms — Higher Intent)
**Budget:** $10,000/month | ABO recommended (see rationale below)
**Estimated CPL Range:** $95–$145

---

### Objective Rationale
Lead Gen with Instant Forms is the right call here. The ICP (VP Sales at 100–500 person SaaS companies) is identifiable on Meta through job title + employer industry targeting, but this cohort rarely clicks off-platform to fill out an external form during a social browsing session. Instant Forms capture intent in-feed with zero friction. We configure the form as 'Higher Intent' (includes a review step) to filter out accidental submissions. The trade-off vs. a Conversions campaign: slightly lower lead quality ceiling, but 2–3x higher volume at this budget level. We recommend running a 30-day test against a parallel Conversions ad set once pixel data reaches 50 events/week.

---

### Ad Set Structure (ABO)

#### Ad Set 1: Cold | VP Sales | B2B SaaS Interest Stack
- **Audience Type:** Cold
- **Targeting:** Job titles: VP Sales, VP of Sales, Head of Sales, Director of Sales AND Industries: Computer Software, Internet, SaaS (via employer industry). Additional layer: Behaviors = 'Business Decision Makers.' Exclude: existing customers (upload suppression list).
- **Geography:** United States + Canada
- **Estimated Audience Size:** 680k–1.1M
- **Budget Allocation:** 70% = $7,000/month ($233/day)
- **Placements:** Facebook Feed, Instagram Feed, Facebook Stories, Instagram Stories. Audience Network: EXCLUDED.
- **Frequency Cap:** 2 impressions/user/week
- **Ad Variants Assigned:** Variant A (guess-which-accounts), Variant B (30%-time-researching), Variant C (buying-signals)

#### Ad Set 2: Lookalike | 1% LAL from Customer List
- **Audience Type:** Lookalike
- **Targeting:** 1% Lookalike audience built from uploaded closed-won customer list (filter to ACV > $10k). Exclude: existing customers, website visitors from last 90 days.
- **Geography:** United States + Canada
- **Estimated Audience Size:** ~2.1M (1% of US/CA combined)
- **Budget Allocation:** 10% = $1,000/month ($33/day)
- **Placements:** Facebook Feed, Instagram Feed. Audience Network: EXCLUDED.
- **Ad Variants Assigned:** Variant B (SDR time-saving angle), Variant C (buying signals angle)
- **Note:** Promote to 20% budget if CPL comes in 30%+ below Cold ad set after 3 weeks.

#### Ad Set 3: Retargeting | Pricing + Demo Page Visitors (90 days)
- **Audience Type:** Retargeting
- **Targeting:** Pixel visitors who visited /pricing or /demo in the last 90 days AND did NOT convert (exclude Lead pixel event). Minimum pool required: 1,000 users — verify in Audience Manager before launch.
- **Geography:** United States + Canada
- **Estimated Audience Size:** 1,200–4,500 (estimate; depends on current site traffic)
- **Budget Allocation:** 20% = $2,000/month ($67/day)
- **Placements:** Facebook Feed, Instagram Feed, Instagram Stories. Audience Network: EXCLUDED.
- **Frequency Cap:** 4 impressions/user/week
- **Ad Variants Assigned:** Variant A (urgency/proof angle — recommend adding a customer quote or stat), Variant B (demo CTA direct)

---

### CBO vs. ABO Recommendation
**Use ABO (Ad Set Budget Optimization).**

Rationale: This campaign spans three strategically distinct audience types — cold (volume), lookalike (quality), and retargeting (intent). With CBO, Meta's algorithm will almost always over-allocate to the cold ad set because it has the most daily impressions available. The retargeting pool ($2k/month) would be under-served or receive erratic spend. ABO gives you guaranteed daily spend on the retargeting segment, which is your highest-intent cohort. Re-evaluate for CBO only if you consolidate to 2 cold ad sets with similar audience types and want Meta to pick between them.

---

### Pixel Events Configuration
Verify all events are firing via Meta Pixel Helper before campaign launch:
1. **Lead** — fire on Instant Form submission confirmation
2. **ViewContent** — fire on /pricing, /demo, and /case-studies page loads
3. **InitiateCheckout** — fire when user begins trial or checkout flow (future use)
4. **Purchase** — fire on subscription confirmation page (when applicable)

Use Event Deduplication via the Conversions API + browser pixel combo to prevent double-counting leads.

---

### Ad Variant Summary
| Variant ID | Headline Angle | Format | CTA |
|---|---|---|---|
| AD-001 | Stop guessing which accounts to call | Static image (dark bg, bold stat) | Get Free Access |
| AD-002 | 30% of SDR time lost to research | Video (15s screen record demo) | See How It Works |
| AD-003 | Buying signals before competitors | Static image (graph/chart creative) | Book a Demo |

All three variants run in Ad Sets 1 and 2. Ad Sets 3 (retargeting) runs AD-001 and AD-002 only — suppress AD-003 from retargeting until you have a dedicated re-engagement angle.

---

### Launch Checklist
1. Verify Meta Pixel is installed on all landing pages and firing Lead event correctly
2. Upload customer suppression list as a Custom Audience (exclude from cold + LAL ad sets)
3. Build 1% LAL audience from customer list — confirm minimum 100 customers in seed
4. Verify retargeting audience pool is >= 1,000 users before activating Ad Set 3
5. Set up Instant Form with 3 qualifying questions: Company Name, Company Size (dropdown), Primary Challenge (dropdown)
6. Enable Conversions API alongside browser pixel for server-side deduplication
7. Set ABO budgets exactly as specified; double-check daily amounts
8. Exclude Audience Network at ad set level for ALL three ad sets
9. Submit ads for review — allow 24–48 hours before scheduled launch date
10. Set up automated rule: pause any ad with CPL > $250 after $300 spend

---

### Notes on Audience Size
The cold ad set is estimated at 680k–1.1M, which clears the 500k minimum threshold. If Meta's Audience Manager shows a number below 500k after building the targeting, loosen the industry filter (remove one industry) or expand job title list to include 'Sales Director' and 'Chief Revenue Officer.'
